flow_points: Sri Lanka started strongly, scoring 58 runs for 2 wickets in the Powerplay (RR 9.67).
P Rathnayake anchored Sri Lanka's innings, contributing 26.7% of the team's runs.
Sri Lanka finished their innings with a high-scoring rate of 15.8 in Death Overs, shifting match momentum.
Oman struggled significantly in their Powerplay, losing 3 wickets for only 36 runs (RR 6.0) and failing to build a stable foundation.
Mohammad Nadeem attempted to anchor Oman's chase with 53(60), contributing 44.2% of their total runs.
M Theekshana delivered a crucial spell of 4.0o-11r-2w, applying sustained defensive pressure.
DN Wellalage supported, delivering a crucial spell of 4.0o-17r-1w, maintaining pressure on the Oman batters.
Oman struggled with boundary frequency, hitting one every 13.3 balls, which increased pressure during the chase.
Under extreme chase pressure (Index: 87.0), Oman experienced a late-innings collapse.
Sri Lanka won convincingly by 105 runs.
